options.content:
# W&B Run Ingestion

Answers **"my run isn't showing data / my logging is delayed."** Use this when a customer says charts are empty, history/metrics lag behind the training job, or `wandb.log()` seems stuck.

Data from a running job flows in as **filestream** HTTP requests, gets parsed into **lines and chunks**, is queued, and is published/consumed through **Kafka** before landing in the datastores. This dashboard walks that path left-to-right so you can find where ingestion is backing up.

For the datastores themselves (MySQL, ClickHouse, object storage) see [W&B Data-stores](/d/wandb-datastores); for Kafka broker health and container resources see [W&B Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance).

Use the **W&B Namespace** dropdown above to pick the customer's install.

panels.title: Filestream HTTP request rate by status
panels.description: TODO: empty until gorilla emits an http.route label — only `/ping` is set today, so the `file_stream` filter matches nothing. The unsampled filestream signal is DogStatsD (handle_line / filestream_new_cells, below), which needs GORILLA_STATSD_PORT>0 (currently 0).

options.content:
**What it shows** — requests to the filestream endpoints, split by status. Filestream is the SDK's main ingest path — every `wandb.log()` from a running job lands here.

**What bad looks like** — a `5xx` spike means writes are being rejected (active runs will see SDK warnings and data will lag or drop). A `2xx` drop to near-zero means jobs can't reach the API at all (ingress / auth / pod crash).

**Do next** — 5xx → check the processing and Kafka sections below, then API pod logs and [W&B Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance). No traffic at all → confirm the customer's job is actually running and can reach the endpoint.

panels.title: Filestream p95 latency
panels.description: TODO: empty until gorilla emits an http.route label — only `/ping` is set today, so the `file_stream` filter matches nothing. For per-operation latency in the meantime, see 'p95 latency by operation' on [W&B API](/d/wandb-api) (spanmetrics span_name).

options.content:
**What it shows** — how long filestream write requests take at p95. Slow filestream = the SDK spends longer per flush, so logged data appears in the UI later.

**What bad looks like** — p95 above ~3s sustained. If latency is high but status codes are still 2xx, the write is succeeding slowly — usually a downstream backup (queue / Kafka / datastore), not a hard failure.

**Do next** — check the **Queue & Kafka lag** section below; if those are climbing, follow to [W&B Data-stores](/d/wandb-datastores) and Kafka on [W&B Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance).

panels.title: Line processing rate (handle_line, filestream_new_cells)
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Rate of ingest lines/cells parsed from filestream payloads. Filtered by namespace="$namespace".

options.content:
**What it shows** — how fast the API is parsing incoming filestream payloads into individual log lines / new cells.

**What bad looks like** — this rate dropping toward zero while filestream requests are still arriving means parsing has stalled — data is coming in but not being processed.

**Do next** — if line processing has stalled, check the error rates in the next panels and the queue/Kafka section below. If those look healthy but processing is still stuck, capture a snapshot and escalate.

panels.title: History chunks processed vs errors
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Processing of history (metrics) file chunks and its error counter. Filtered by namespace="$namespace".

options.content:
**What it shows** — the rate of history (metrics) file chunks processed, alongside the error counter. History is what backs the run's charts.

**What bad looks like** — any sustained `error` line, or `processed` collapsing while requests keep arriving. Errors here directly explain "my charts are missing data."

**Do next** — if errors are non-zero, Open Traces in Explore (top-right) for the filestream service, and check [W&B Data-stores](/d/wandb-datastores) (history is written to ClickHouse). Capture a snapshot and escalate if the cause isn't obvious.

panels.title: Events chunks processed vs errors
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Processing of events (system metrics) file chunks and its error counter. Filtered by namespace="$namespace".

options.content:
**What it shows** — the rate of events (system-metrics) file chunks processed, alongside its error counter. Same shape as history, different data type.

**What bad looks like** — any sustained `error` line, or `processed` dropping to zero while ingest traffic continues.

**Do next** — treat like the history panel: non-zero errors → drill to a trace for the filestream service and check [W&B Data-stores](/d/wandb-datastores). Escalate with a snapshot if unclear.

panels.title: Fetch history errors
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Errors reading back run history. Filtered by namespace="$namespace".

options.content:
**What it shows** — errors when the API tries to read back run history (the reverse of ingest — this is what the UI does when loading charts).

**What bad looks like** — any sustained non-zero rate. This maps directly to "the run page won't load its charts" even when ingestion itself is fine.

**Do next** — if firing, the read path to the history store is unhealthy → check [W&B Data-stores](/d/wandb-datastores) (ClickHouse) and the API error panels on [W&B API & GraphQL](/d/wandb-api). Capture a snapshot and escalate if the store looks healthy.

panels.title: Filemeta queue depth
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Gauge: items waiting in the filemeta processing queue. Filtered by namespace="$namespace".

options.content:
**What it shows** — how many file-metadata items are waiting in the processing queue right now (a gauge, not a rate).

**What bad looks like** — a queue depth that keeps climbing instead of hovering near a steady level = ingestion is backed up; work is arriving faster than it drains.

**Do next** — a rising queue points downstream: check the Kafka lag panels below, then [W&B Data-stores](/d/wandb-datastores) and Kafka on [W&B Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance).

panels.title: Kafka run-update inflight records (consumer lag proxy)
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Gauge: records read from Kafka but not yet processed — a consumer-lag proxy. Filtered by namespace="$namespace".

options.content:
**What it shows** — records pulled from Kafka but not yet processed. This is the best proxy you have for **consumer lag**: how far behind the consumer is falling.

**What bad looks like** — inflight climbing and not draining = the consumer can't keep up with the topic → run updates land late, so the UI lags behind the job.

**Do next** — rising inflight → check the publish/subscribe rates and latency below, then Kafka broker health on [W&B Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance). If the consumer is starved by a slow datastore, see [W&B Data-stores](/d/wandb-datastores).

panels.title: Kafka run-update publish / subscribe rates
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). Publish success vs consumer process/retry/already-processed rates for the run-update topic. Filtered by namespace="$namespace".

options.content:
**What it shows** — the producer side (`publish_success`) vs the consumer side (`subscribe_process`), plus `subscribe_retry` and `already_processed`, for the run-update Kafka topic.

**What bad looks like** — `publish_success` sustained well above `subscribe_process` = producing faster than consuming (lag builds). A climbing `subscribe_retry` = the consumer keeps failing and re-attempting.

**Do next** — a publish/subscribe gap or high retries → check Kafka broker health and consumer resources on [W&B Managed Install Performance](/d/wandb-managed-install-performance), and the datastore the consumer writes to on [W&B Data-stores](/d/wandb-datastores).

panels.title: Kafka run-update subscribe process latency
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). If this is a DogStatsD timing/histogram it may expose _bucket/_sum/_count instead — adjust to histogram_quantile once confirmed. Filtered by namespace="$namespace".

options.content:
**What it shows** — how long the consumer takes to process each run-update record it pulls from Kafka.

**What bad looks like** — latency trending up over time. Slow per-record processing is the usual cause of rising inflight/consumer lag: the consumer is working but each item takes too long (typically a slow datastore write).

**Do next** — rising latency alongside climbing inflight above → the bottleneck is downstream of Kafka: check [W&B Data-stores](/d/wandb-datastores). Capture a snapshot and escalate if the datastore looks healthy.

**What it shows** — how to get from "ingestion is slow/failing" to the actual slow or failing request.

**Do next** — click **Open Traces in Explore** (top-right). In the Jaeger query, set `service.name` to the filestream / gorilla ingest service, and filter `duration>3s` (for slow ingest) or `error=true` (for failed writes), scoped to the same time window as the spike you saw above. Open a matching trace and read the span chain — it shows where the time or error is (parsing, Kafka publish, or the datastore write).

**Note** — traces are **sampled**, so use them to find *a representative example*, not to count volume; the metric panels above are the source of truth for how often it happens.
